title: Coffee on Linen
stainChemistry: Coffee = melanoidins + chlorogenic acid + tannin polyphenols. Coffee with cream adds milk casein + butterfat. On linen (cellulose fiber, similar to cotton in chemistry but shrinks more aggressively + dyes bleed easily + structured pieces have stiffeners that warp wet), the tannin chemistry transfers from cotton (no alkaline, no hot water), but the FIBER cautions are stricter: hot water shrinks linen significantly, dyed linen bleeds easily, structured linen (suits, blazers, table linens with built-in stiffeners) loses shape when wet. Standard tannin protocol (cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar) works on flat unstructured linen. Anything with structure / lining / trim = pro.
whyThisWorks: Same tannin chemistry as coffee-cotton: cold water + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar. Linen fiber tolerates this on simple flat pieces (napkins, basic shirts, simple skirts). For structured linen, the wet treatment risks shape change AND dyed-linen bleed. Honest fork: simple linen = standard protocol; structured linen = pro.
defaultAssumption: Linen napkin / shirt / simple piece = home protocol. Linen suit / lined dress / structured table runner / dry-clean-only label = pro from minute one.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Check care label: dry-clean-only = pro. Look for structured features (lining, interfacing, trim, embroidery). Structured / lined linen = pro. Simple flat linen (napkin, basic shirt) = proceed.
Flip inside out. Run cold water through the BACK. Cold pulls fresh tannin out — the most effective fast move on linen.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water + Dawn on white cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hidden seam test (dyed linen often bleeds). Dab Dawn + cold water mix on cloth, press 2 min. Color transfer = pro lane (linen dye instability). Clean = proceed.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dawn + cold water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dawn in cold water. Press cloth on stain 5 min. Blot. NO OxiClean — alkaline darkens tannin permanently.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz in warm water
spottingProtocol.instruction: If cream coffee: enzyme pretreat in WARM (not hot) water on damp cloth, 15 min. Skip for black.
Hidden test on dyed linen first. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min. Don't mix with bleach / ammonia / alkaline.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1 tbsp vinegar + 1 cup cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar rinse 5 min. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps tannin light.
Cool wash per care label — NEVER hot (linen shrinks aggressively). Air-DRY FLAT — never tumble-dry until fully clear (heat shrinks + sets tannin).

steps: 1. Care label + structure check. Structured = pro.
8. Cool wash + air-dry flat. Inspect before dryer.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold throughout — linen shrinks under hot water, fiber denatures
warnings: NEVER hot water on linen — shrinks aggressively + sets tannin
NEVER alkaline cleaners on tannin — darkens permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on dyed linen — strips dye + yellows white
NEVER tumble-dry until fully clear — heat shrinks + sets tannin
Structured / lined / trimmed linen = pro (shape change + bleed risk)
Hidden-area dye test mandatory on dyed linen

homeSolutions.agent: Structured Linen Goes to Pro; Simple Linen Stays Home
homeSolutions.instruction: Linen has two cases. SIMPLE LINEN (napkins, basic shirts, plain skirts) = standard tannin home protocol works. STRUCTURED LINEN (suits, lined dresses, table runners with stiffeners, anything with embroidery / trim / dry-clean-only label) = pro from minute one. Wet treatment on structured linen risks shape change AND dyed-linen bleeds easily. Check the care label first.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Back-Flush — The Most Effective Fast Move
homeSolutions.instruction: Flip the linen inside out. Run cold tap water through the BACK of the stain for 1–2 minutes. Cold pulls fresh coffee out — works especially well on linen because the fiber is open-weave.

homeSolutions.agent: Hidden Patch Test — Dyed Linen Bleeds Easily
homeSolutions.instruction: If the linen is colored, dab a drop of Dawn + cold water on a hidden seam, press 2 min, blot. Color transfer = pro lane (linen dye is not stable to wet treatment). Clean = proceed. Skip for white linen.

Mix 1 tablespoon Dawn in 2 cups cold water. Work into the spot with a soft toothbrush 5 minutes. Cool rinse. Do NOT use OxiClean or baking soda — alkaline cleaners turn coffee tannin DARKER permanently.
Coffee with cream: dissolve OxiClean Versatile or Biz in WARM water — bath temperature, under 100°F. NOT hot. Damp cloth 15 min. Skip for black.
Dab 3% drugstore peroxide directly on remaining stain, 5–10 minutes. On dyed linen — hidden-area test FIRST (linen dye especially fragile). Skip if test shows bleed.
1 tablespoon white vinegar + 1 cup cool water. Apply 5 minutes. Neutralizes peroxide + keeps tannin light. Rinse cool.
Cool wash per care label — NEVER hot. Linen shrinks aggressively under hot water. AIR-DRY FLAT — do NOT tumble-dry until the stain is fully gone. Heat shrinks linen AND sets residual coffee.

steps: 1. Structured linen = pro. Simple linen = home.
2. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach.
4. Hidden-area dye test on colored linen.
5. Dawn + cold water + soft toothbrush, 5 min.
6. Cream-coffee: WARM (not hot) enzyme pretreat, 15 min.
7. 3% peroxide damp cloth, 5–10 min, hidden test.
9. Cool wash + AIR-DRY FLAT. Never tumble-dry until clear.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if linen is structured / lined / trimmed / dry-clean-only, if hidden-area dye test bleeds, or if hot water / dryer was already applied.
warnings: NEVER hot water — linen shrinks aggressively
NEVER chlorine bleach on dyed linen
NEVER tumble-dry until stain is fully clear — heat shrinks + sets tannin
Hidden-area dye test mandatory on colored linen

neverDo: Never hot water on linen — shrinks
Never chlorine bleach on dyed linen
Never wet-treat structured / lined linen at home
fiberSensitivities: Linen shrinks aggressively under hot water — cool only
Dyed linen bleeds easily — hidden-area test mandatory
Structured linen warps under wet treatment — pro
Heat sets coffee on linen permanently
Vintage / antique linen = pro for any treatment

materialWarnings: Structured / lined linen = pro from minute one
Cool water only — linen shrinks under heat
Air-dry flat — heat shrinks AND sets coffee
Dyed linen dye is fragile — hidden test always

escalation.when: Structured / lined / trimmed linen, dry-clean-only label, dyed linen failed hidden test, hot water / dryer used previously, vintage linen.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee on [simple / structured / dyed / vintage] linen, [black / with cream], age [hours / days], home-treated with cold + Dawn + 3% peroxide + vinegar + cool wash. No hot water / no alkaline / no chlorine bleach. [Residual / structured concerns]."

contextQuestions: Simple flat linen or structured (lined / trimmed)?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Vintage linen is fragile fiber-wise — cool everything. Structured pieces (linen suits) need professional finishing after any wet treatment. Dyed linen bleeds — pre-applied wet treatment may have already caused bleed; disclose.
